output: Implemented the approved plan and captured execution evidence; full-suite green verification remains blocked by unrelated out-of-scope test failures.

The required full Vitest rerun surfaced failures outside the approved refactor boundary, including `useTasksPaneController.test.ts`, `PanesContext.test.tsx`, `useDeleteGenerationAction.test.ts`, `GenerationTokenPanel.test.tsx`, `taskCreation.test.ts`, `taskParamContract.test.ts`, `AppProviders.test.tsx`, and `VideoEditorProvider.test.tsx`; those were left unchanged to avoid unrelated scope creep, so T5 is reported as skipped rather than done.
The requested checkpoint file did not exist, so it was created at `.megaplan/plans/refactor-the-phase-config-20260414-0017/execution_checkpoint.json` with the current task and sense-check state.

task_updates.executor_notes: Verified that `handleGenerationTypeModeChange` now computes the builtin phase config inline and writes `generationTypeMode`, `selectedPhasePresetId`, `phaseConfig`, and model frame substate in one `updateFields` call for both branches. Basic mode sets the mode default preset ID, advanced mode explicitly clears it to `null`, and `handleRestoreDefaults` now updates preset ID plus `phaseConfig` atomically. The forced `rebuildPhaseConfig` call on mode switch was removed.

Verified that `usePresetAutoSelect` now only resets on settings loading and auto-selects the builtin default on initial mount or shot navigation when a basic-mode shot has no preset selected. Previous-mode tracking and mode-change auto-select logic were removed, and the hook signature cleanup was propagated through `useMotionControlPresetState` and `MotionControl`. Scoped vitest passed for the motion preset hook and `MotionControl`.

Verified that `VideoGenerationModalSections` now uses provider-backed `handleGenerationTypeModeChange`, `handlePhasePresetSelect`, `handlePhasePresetRemove`, and `handlePhaseConfigChange` instead of raw `updateField` calls for those paths. The modal sections test was updated to assert the provider handlers are invoked from both `BatchSettingsForm` and `MotionControl` surfaces.

Confirmed `rebuildPhaseConfig` is now called only from `handleAmountOfMotionChange` and `handleSelectedLorasChange`, which are basic-mode-only incremental sync paths and do not reintroduce the dual-writer problem. Removed the unused `force` parameter and stale comments from the helper, then reran the related motion/preset modal test modules.

Verified `./node_modules/.bin/tsc --noEmit` passes with no type errors. Ran a short temporary repro script against the dual-writer surface; it passed and was deleted after execution. Scoped Vitest for `src/tools/travel-between-images` now passes after updating stale expectations and mocks in `Header.test.tsx`, `BatchModeContent.test.tsx`, `useVideoGenerationModalController.test.tsx`, and `useTimelineOrchestrator.hook.test.tsx`. The required full `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run --reporter=verbose` rerun still fails in unrelated suites outside the approved refactor surface, including `useTasksPaneController.test.ts`, `PanesContext.test.tsx`, `useDeleteGenerationAction.test.ts`, `GenerationTokenPanel.test.tsx`, `taskCreation.test.ts`, `taskParamContract.test.ts`, `AppProviders.test.tsx`, and `VideoEditorProvider.test.tsx`, so branch-wide green verification remains blocked by non-scope failures.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Confirmed in `useVideoTravelSettingsHandlers` that the basic-mode branch writes `generationTypeMode`, `selectedPhasePresetId`, and `phaseConfig` in the same `updateFields` call, and the trailing mode-change `rebuildPhaseConfig` call is gone.

Confirmed the advanced-mode branch writes `selectedPhasePresetId: null` together with `generationTypeMode` and `phaseConfig`, and `handleRestoreDefaults` now performs a single atomic `updateFields` write.

Confirmed `usePresetAutoSelect` no longer contains `prevGenerationTypeModeRef`, `prevHasStructureVideoRef`, or `modeChanged` logic. The initial mount and shot-navigation auto-select path remains, and the preserved integration is covered by the scoped motion preset hook test.

Confirmed `VideoGenerationModalSections` routes generation-type changes, preset select/remove, and phase-config edits through provider handlers. The updated modal sections test asserts those provider handlers are called.

Confirmed `tsc --noEmit` passes and the scoped travel-between-images suite now passes cleanly. The required full Vitest rerun did not pass because of unrelated existing failures outside the refactor surface, so branch-wide green verification is still blocked.
